appliedUpdates.description: Added a factual architecture note that GraphShell intentionally unmounts unfocused surfaces, so any roadmap/tokenomics state that must survive cross-surface handoffs needs to live above that boundary (for example in a shell-level persistence provider).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refine the `frontend-experience-worker` handoff rubric so `followedProcedure=true` either requires naming a pre-change failing expectation verbatim or explicitly allows transcript-backed test-first evidence when the handoff test inventory is otherwise complete.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `graph-shell-sitewide-atmosphere-and-mobile-overlay-consistency-polish` and `graph-shell-cross-surface-context-persistence-polish` appear to have written failing tests before implementation, but their handoffs still omitted a named failing expectation while reporting `followedProcedure=true`, leaving review outcomes dependent on transcript interpretation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify immersive-layer provenance requirements for follow-up fixes that only extend already-vendored Radiant/Hermes implementations, including whether reuse-only fixes must restate exact upstream URLs/files or may reference the earlier provenance record.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `graph-shell-sitewide-atmosphere-and-mobile-overlay-consistency-polish` legitimately reused the shipped `HeroShaderCanvas` and `AsciiCanvasRenderer`, but the current skill text still reads as though every immersive-layer handoff must restate exact upstream provenance, making `followedProcedure=true` ambiguous for reuse-only fixes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update scrutiny-review/synthesis guidance to distinguish original feature ownership from regressions reintroduced by later stacked features so rerun blocker attribution stays historically accurate.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The hero CTA blocker was attributed in round 1 to `graph-shell-navigation-and-node-first-exploration`, but review of `graph-shell-hero-cta-node-first-destination-polish` showed that feature had already moved the CTA to `#graph-proof` and a later commit in `graph-shell-cross-surface-handoffs-and-context` reintroduced `#proof`.
